latest.title: Exploring Amazon S3 Tables with PyIceberg - Part 3: Schema Evolution and Table Maintenance
latest.description: Learn how to perform Iceberg schema evolution, adding columns, renaming fields, widening types, and dropping columns entirely through metadata-only operations using PyIceberg and Amazon S3 Tables, with no Spark cluster or data rewrites required. Discover how S3 Tables automatically handles critical table maintenance like compaction, snapshot expiry, and orphan file cleanup, keeping your Iceberg tables healthy without manual intervention.

latest.title: What Designing a 135-Tool Architecture Taught Me About AWS (and Myself)
latest.description: I built an AI-powered game generator with eight agents and 135 tool functions, and it forced me to confront the gap between a working prototype and a scalable system. AWS Builder Center's workshops and community helped me bridge it.

latest.title: From Localhost to the Cloud: How My First AWS Architecture Changed the Way I Think About Code
latest.description: I share how deploying my satellite imagery ML platform on AWS transformed the way I think about software engineering, from writing my first Terraform configuration to discovering that cloud architecture actually makes you write better application code.

latest.title: Hide regions and services in the AWS console
latest.description: You can now show only the regions and services on the AWS management console

latest.title: The Cloud Isn't Complicated!
latest.description: Cloud Computing sounds scary and technical, but I promise it is a lot more simple than it sounds.

latest.title: AIdeas Finalist: VIGIA, The RoadIntelligence IDE
latest.description: VIGIA is the nervous system of the sentient city. By transforming everyday vehicles into a decentralized sensor network, it feeds the pulse of the road into an autonomous AI brain. VIGIA doesn’t just detect hazards, it actively plans repair logistics and dispatches city crews, turning static concrete into a living, self-healing ecosystem.

latest.title: Building an Internal Developer Platform with Kubernetes, Docker, and GitOps on AWS
latest.description: Modern engineering teams are under constant pressure to deliver faster without compromising reliability. One of the most effective ways to achieve this is by implementing an Internal Developer Platform (IDP)—a self-service layer that abstracts infrastructure complexity and empowers developers to focus on building features.

latest.description: Model Once, Run Anywhere with Shared Kubernetes Infrastructure

latest.description: A guide for student developers on navigating the AWS ecosystem, from managing the Free Tier to leveraging student-specific resources like AWS Educate.

latest.title: Secure Agent Sandboxes for Programmatic Tool Calling on AWS: Firecracker and gVisor on EKS
latest.description: Building isolated code execution environments for AI agents that write and run untrusted Python with inline tool calling

latest.title: Beyond the Pilot: Why the "Frontier Firm" Needs a Graph Intelligence Layer
latest.description: We are moving away from simple "Chatbots" toward "Agentic Ecosystems." However, there is a looming bottleneck: Knowledge Fragmentation.

latest.title: Building a Secure, Scalable Platform on AWS
latest.description: Modern cloud-native applications demand automation, security, and scalability from day one. This architecture demonstrates how to combine GitOps, Infrastructure as Code, and managed Kubernetes to build a production-ready delivery platform on AWS.

latest.title: Building a Scalable Serverless Workflow on AWS
latest.description: Modern applications demand scalability, resilience, and cost efficiency—and serverless architecture delivers exactly that. In this post, we’ll walk through a powerful event-driven workflow built entirely on Amazon Web Services using core managed services.

latest.title: The Art of Hybrid Development: Optimizing Application Lifecycles with Kiro Pro and AWS
latest.description: A technical deep-dive based on real-world implementation experiences, detailing a high-performance workflow using Kiro Pro. We explore how to effectively segment the development process: from architecture and code generation in IDE environments (Windows) to rigorous deployment, least-privilege analysis, and automated technical documentation via CLI (Linux). This is designed for engineers looking to maximize the efficiency of AWS MCPs and advanced LLMs.

latest.title: Builder Challenge: May 2026
latest.description: Will you be one of the three lucky winners who submits the hidden AWS service in the word cloud?

latest.title: Updating encryption in S3 replication using newly released UpdateObjectEncryption API
latest.description: In S3 replication and batch replication, if data in source bucket is under SSE-S3, the replica in the destination bucket will still be under SSE-S3 even if the destination bucket has SSE-KMS encryption enabled. What if your customer requires that data in destination bucket must be encrypted with SSE-KMS while the source bucket is owned by another entity and cannot switch to  SSE-KMS? This article addresses such scenario using the newly released S3 API: UpdateObjectEncryption.

latest.title: Lost in the AI Hype, I Started Small
latest.description: Returning to tech after maternity leave felt overwhelming, so I started small by building a portfolio site to consolidate 80+ pieces of content scattered across platforms since 2015. Using AWS Kiro (an AI-powered IDE) for the first time, I built the entire project through conversation—no complex setup needed.

trending.description: Crea tu primer proyecto con Strands Agents en Nerdearla del 17 y 18 de Abril 2026 en Centro GAM, Santiago, Chile.

trending.title: AIdeas Finalist: Caligo Dynamics - AI Powered Autonomous Drone Swarm for Emergency Response
trending.description: When a fire breaks out, firefighters often enter buildings blindly, unable to see victims, hazards, or even the way out. This project uses AI-powered drone swarms built with ROS2, PX4, Kiro, and Amazon Bedrock to detect fires, locate civilians, map dangerous environments, and help emergency teams contain fires before lives are lost.

trending.title: Revolutionizing Email Productivity with AI-Powered Automation through CLI
trending.description: As a developer facing email overload, I built a solution for the Q Developer Challenge #1: email management. What began as a simple CLI tool grew into an AI-powered system that has transformed my workflow.

trending.title: Amazon S3 Files: from Kafka to S3 via NFS
trending.description: A hands-on demo of Amazon S3 Files with Confluent Cloud Kafka, Lambda, and AWS CDK. I show how Kafka events can be written to an S3-backed NFS mount using normal filesystem calls, what CDK still gets wrong today, and how cold vs warm reads behave for small and large files in real runs.

trending.title: 10,000 AIdeas Competition Terms (Spanish)
trending.description: Terms for the 10,000 AIdeas Competition, in Spanish Language

trending.title: El problema de los Small Files: Guía de optimización en S3
trending.description: Descubre por qué el exceso de archivos pequeños destruye el rendimiento de Spark, Glue y Athena. Aprende estrategias de compactación, control de particionamiento y optimización de tamaño (128MB-512MB) para reducir costos y acelerar tus pipelines de datos hasta 10 veces.

trending.title: AI won't replace developers — but ignoring it will cost you
trending.description: Practical insights and a live demo on how AI tools like Kiro are reshaping the daily workflow of tech professionals, presented at the AWS User Group Rio Grande do Sul meetup.

trending.title: Strands Agents & AgentCore: Two Pieces of the Same Puzzle
trending.description: A practical breakdown of two complementary AWS tools for building AI agents: Strands Agents, the open-source SDK for writing agent logic, and Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, the managed platform for running agents securely in production.

trending.title: Pensar en Serverless para crear bellos sueños 🦋
trending.description: En estas líneas te explico una sencilla arquitectura para desplegar un sitio web íntegramente utilizando servicios Serverless que podría servir, como en mi caso, para mostrarle al mundo mi interés por la ecología (con repositorio github incluido)

trending.title: I Had No Idea AWS Certifications Could Open This Many Doors. Here's What I've Learned So Far.
trending.description: This article shares my personal journey of discovering how AWS certifications can set students apart in the job market, breaking down the certification roadmap from Cloud Practitioner to specialty levels and the free resources available to get started. Whether you're a CS major or just curious about cloud, the best time to start is now.

trending.title: Agentic Branding: Let Kiro Turn Your CV into a Cloud-Native Website
trending.description: In this guide, I am going to use AWS Kiro (the intelligent AI agent) to automate a professional transformation. I'll take your existing LinkedIn profile data and, with just three prompts, have Kiro build a modern, cloud-native static portfolio website using Next.js and Tailwind CSS and deploy it on S3.

trending.title: The Git habits that made reviewers trust me before they read a single line of code
trending.description: How a structured branching strategy, conventional commits, and knowing when to use cherry-pick instead of a full merge helped me stand out when contributing to projects on GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps, and AWS CodeCommit.

trending.title: Everything AWS Gives Students for Free (And How I Actually Used It)
trending.description: A practical breakdown of every AWS tool available to students for free: $200 credits, Amazon Bedrock, Q Developer, Builder Center workshops, and Cloud Clubs. Includes real code snippets and how I've used each one in projects at Michigan State.

trending.title: Build Address Autocomplete and Store Locator Features for Your Retail Application with Amazon Location Service
trending.description: Learn how to build checkout address autocomplete and store locator features for retail applications using Amazon Location Service and ASP.NET Core. This post walks through the Suggest, GetPlace, and ReverseGeocode APIs with a working BFF architecture that keeps credentials server-side and serves a clean API to your storefront.

trending.title: S3 Vectors: How to build a RAG without a vector database
trending.description: Amazon S3 now stores and queries vectors natively. Here's how to build a RAG pipeline with just S3 and Bedrock, no Pinecone, no OpenSearch, no infrastructure

trending.title: Powertools for AWS Lambda - Best Practices By Default
trending.description: Boost Lambda efficiency with AWS Powertools: structured logs, tracing, metrics, and more. Essential for serverless observability.
